flags.concern: Bulk editing: nested `ClipMeta` patches are shallow-merged, so the plan's assumption that `handleUpdateClips` already supports bulk `text`/`entrance`/`exit`/`continuous` updates is incorrect.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:89-105` applies the exact same patch object to every selected clip, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:220-239` only does a top-level spread merge. In the existing single-clip UI, nested fields are preserved manually per clip (`src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:126-183` and `:275-295`). A bulk patch like `{ text: { fontSize: 48 } }` would overwrite each clip's existing text content/color/alignment.

flags.concern: Bulk panel tabs: the proposed visibility rules diverge from the actual single-clip behavior, so the bulk panel would lose parity for common selections.
flags.evidence: The plan says to show Audio only when any selected clip is on an audio track, but `getVisibleClipTabs` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:37-49` shows the Audio tab for non-text clips on visual tracks as well. A multi-selection of ordinary video clips on visual tracks would incorrectly lose bulk volume controls under the proposed rule.

flags.concern: Properties parity: the plan equates parity with mirroring `ClipPanel`, but the repository's single-clip properties UI does not currently expose crop or transition controls, even though the idea explicitly calls those out.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:119-305` only renders effects, timing, position, audio, and text controls. Crop reset/fill lives in the overlay editor instead (`src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:700-741`), and `rg -n "transition" src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor src/tools/video-editor/hooks` found no transition-editing UI. Mirroring `ClipPanel` will still miss requested crop/transition parity.

flags.concern: Context menu: the plan drops requested multi-copy support even though the idea explicitly includes copy and the repository has no existing clip copy/paste workflow to fall back on.
flags.evidence: The idea calls out context-menu copy for multi-selection, but the plan's assumptions mark copy/paste out of scope. In the current implementation, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:143-195` only exposes split, mute/unmute, and delete, and `rg -n "copy|paste|clipboard" src/tools/video-editor` found no clip-level copy/paste flow to preserve parity with.

flags.concern: Validation: Scope creep: the plan makes undo behavior a success criterion even though this editor codebase does not currently implement undo/redo.
flags.evidence: The success criteria and manual checklist require undoing bulk edits, but `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:35-96` has no undo/redo handling, and `rg -n "undo|redo" src/tools/video-editor` returned no matches. That acceptance bar is not grounded in the current repository unless undo becomes separate scope.
